text: There is an uncanny silence here, broken only by the distant hum of electronics and the glow of emergency lights. The air feels tense, as if the usual order of things has been paused. I feel a mix of curiosity and unease, the kind that invites contemplation about liminality—spaces between use and abandonment. Am I intruding on the comfortable rhythm of familiarity, or am I discovering the quiet heartbeat of an emptied place?
